briefdescription.para: ARMA9 macro to identify cortexA9.
Writes the given CPU Interface register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
Register offset to be written.
32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XScuGic_CPUWriteReg(XScuGic *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Reads the given CPU Interface register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
Rregister offset to be read.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XScuGic_CPUReadReg(XScuGic *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: Writes the given Distributor Interface register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
Register offset to be written.
32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XScuGic_DistWriteReg(XScuGic *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Reads the given Distributor Interface register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
Register offset to be read.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XScuGic_DistReadReg(XScuGic *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: Config table.

detaileddescription.para: The XScuGic driver must know when to acknowledge the interrupt. The entry which specifies this as a bit mask where each bit corresponds to a specific interrupt. A bit set indicates to ACK it before servicing it. Generally, acknowledge before service is used when the interrupt signal is edge-sensitive, and after when the signal is level-sensitive.
Refer to the XScuGic_Config data structure in xscugic.h for details on how this table should be initialized.

briefdescription.para: Makes the connection between the Int_Id of the interrupt source and the associated handler that is to run when the interrupt is recognized.

detaileddescription.para: The argument provided in this call as the Callbackref is used as the argument for the handler when it is called.

InstancePtr
Pointer to the XScuGic instance.
Int_Id
contains the ID of the interrupt source and should be in the range of 0 to XSCUGIC_MAX_NUM_INTR_INPUTS - 1
Handler
Handler for interrupt.
CallBackRef
Instance pointer of the connecting driver.
- XST_SUCCESS if the handler was connected correctly.
WARNING: The handler provided as an argument will overwrite any handler that was previously connected.

briefdescription.para: Updates the interrupt table with the Null Handler and NULL arguments at the location pointed at by the Int_Id.

detaileddescription.para: This effectively disconnects that interrupt source from any handler. The interrupt is disabled also.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XScuGic instance to be worked on.
Contains the ID of the interrupt source and should be in the range of 0 to XSCUGIC_MAX_NUM_INTR_INPUTS - 1

briefdescription.para: Enables the interrupt source provided as the argument Int_Id.

detaileddescription.para: Any pending interrupt condition for the specified Int_Id will occur after this function is called. This API also maps the interrupt to the requesting CPU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XScuGic instance.
Contains the ID of the interrupt source and should be in the range of 0 to XSCUGIC_MAX_NUM_INTR_INPUTS - 1

briefdescription.para: Disables the interrupt source provided as the argument Int_Id such that the interrupt controller will not cause interrupts for the specified Int_Id.

detaileddescription.para: The interrupt controller will continue to hold an interrupt condition for the Int_Id, but will not cause an interrupt. This API also unmaps the interrupt for the requesting CPU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XScuGic instance.
Contains the ID of the interrupt source and should be in the range of 0 to XSCUGIC_MAX_NUM_INTR_INPUTS - 1

briefdescription.para: CfgInitialize a specific interrupt controller instance/driver.

listitem.para: Initialize fields of the XScuGic structure
Initial vector table with stub function calls

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XScuGic instance.
Pointer to a config table for the particular device this driver is associated with.

Device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use Config->BaseAddress for this parameters, passing the physical address instead.

briefdescription.para: Allows software to simulate an interrupt in the interrupt controller.

detaileddescription.para: This function will only be successful when the interrupt controller has been started in simulation mode. A simulated interrupt allows the interrupt controller to be tested without any device to drive an interrupt input signal into it.

InstancePtr
Pointer to the XScuGic instance.
Int_Id
Software interrupt ID to simulate an interrupt.
Cpu_Identifier
List of CPUs to send the interrupt. For VERSAL_NET bits 0-7 specifies core ID to send the interrupt. bits 8-15 specifies the cluster id.
XST_SUCCESS if successful, or XST_FAILURE if the interrupt could not be simulated

briefdescription.para: Gets the interrupt priority and trigger type for the specificd IRQ source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
IRQ source number to modify.
Pointer to the value of the priority of the IRQ source. This is a return value.
Pointer to the value of the trigger of the IRQ source.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the value of the trigger of the IRQ source.

memberdef.argsstring: (XScuGic *InstancePtr, u32 Int_Id, u8 Priority, u8 Trigger)
memberdef.name: XScuGic_SetPriorityTriggerType

briefdescription.para: Sets the interrupt priority and trigger type for the specificd IRQ source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
IRQ source number to modify.
New priority for the IRQ source. 0 is highest priority, 0xF8(248) is lowest. There are 32 priority levels supported with a step of 8. Hence the supported priorities are 0, 8, 16, 32, 40 ..., 248.

New trigger type for the IRQ source. Each bit pair describes the configuration for an INT_ID. SFI Read Only b10 always PPI Read Only depending on how the PPIs are configured. b01 Active HIGH level sensitive b11 Rising edge sensitive SPI LSB is read only. b01 Active HIGH level sensitive b11 Rising edge sensitive/

briefdescription.para: Sets the target CPU for the interrupt of a peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
CPU number for which the interrupt has to be targeted For VERSAL_NET APU: 0 t0 3 bits specifies core id and 4 to 7 bits specifies cluster id of the targeted core.
IRQ source number to modify.

briefdescription.para: Unmaps specific SPI interrupt from the target CPU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
CPU number from which the interrupt has to be unmapped. For VERSAL_NET APU: 0 t0 3 bits specifies core id and 4 to 7 bits specifies cluster id of the targeted core.
IRQ source number to modify

briefdescription.para: Unmaps all SPI interrupts from the target CPU.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.
CPU number from which the interrupts has to be unmapped.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the interrupt target register contains all interrupts to be targeted for current CPU.

detaileddescription.para: If they are programmed to be forwarded to current cpu, this API disable all interrupts and disable GIC distributor. This API also removes current CPU from interrupt target registers for all interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Updates the CpuId global variable.

parameterdescription.para: CPU core number.

briefdescription.para: Returns the CpuId variable.
Checks whether the XScGic is initialized or not given the device ID/BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the XScuGic configuration structure for the specified device, or NULL if the device ID/BaseAddress is not found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function is the primary interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to the interrupt source such that it is called when an interrupt of the interrupt controller is active. It will resolve which interrupts are active and enabled and call the appropriate interrupt handler. It uses the Interrupt Type information to determine when to acknowledge the interrupt. Highest priority interrupts are serviced first.
This function assumes that an interrupt vector table has been previously initialized. It does not verify that entries in the table are valid before calling an interrupt handler.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XScuGic instance.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test reads the ID registers and compares them.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XScuGic instance.

para.verbatim: -XST_SUCCESS if self-test is successful. -XST_FAILURE if the self-test is not successful.

briefdescription.para: < prevent circular inclusions
